hero_subtitle: SFTY connects participant data, transaction signals, ownership structures, and reporting workflows into one secure supervisory layer for comprehensive tokenized asset oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Supervision gaps create unclear market views
problem_description: Regulators struggle to connect tokenized asset activity with participant and ownership data across fragmented sources. This leads to delays in risk detection, incomplete evidence, and challenges in producing timely and defensible supervisory reports.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY integrates tokenized asset activity, participant profiles, ownership data, transaction signals and supervisory cases into a connected workflow that enables regulators to analyze risk and prepare evidence-based reports efficiently.
workflow_title: From tokenized asset signals to actions
workflow_description: SFTY connects transaction signals, participant data and ownership links through case workflows to supervisory actions and evidence-based reporting for regulators.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. The supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented tokenized asset data, market signals, ownership context and cases into one audit-ready supervisory environment for clearer regulatory oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators with a secure, evidence-grade platform supporting controlled access, auditability and procurement-safe deployment in tokenized asset supervision environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how tokenized asset supervision workflows operate
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain supervisory case workflows for tokenized asset activity
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports audit of tokenized assets
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration models for tokenized asset data
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for tokenized asset regulators?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators a connected platform to monitor tokenized assets, participant data, transactions, ownership, and reporting workflows within a secure supervisory environment.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support supervisory workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects signals, licensing records, cases and alerts into structured workflows, enabling risk prioritization, investigations and coordinated supervisory actions across tokenized asset markets.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and evidence-grade data to support audit-ready supervisory reviews and defensible reporting for tokenized asset oversight.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing provider data?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects multiple KYT, KYC, KYB, Travel Rule and blockchain analytics providers without replacing them, normalizing their outputs into a consistent supervisory view.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing supervisory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates as a supervisory layer above existing tools and providers, enhancing data orchestration and workflow management without replacing core analytics or compliance systems.
final_description: Review how SFTY connects participant data, transaction signals, ownership, cases and reporting into one audit-ready supervisory layer.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
